text:
Comprehensive Perinatal Service | Kern County, CA
What is CPSP?
CPSP stands for Comprehensive Perinatal Services Program. It gives free support to pregnant women in Kern County who have Medi-Cal. CPSP helps moms stay healthy and gives babies the best start in life.
You can get help:
During your pregnancy
After your baby is born (up to 2 months)
How CPSP helps you
You can get:
Linkage to prenatal care to keep you and your baby healthy
Nutrition tips and free prenatal vitamins
Childbirth classes to help you prepare for your new baby
Support for stress or emotions
Breastfeeding and baby care help
Connections to other programs like WIC, dental care, family planning/birth control, and baby doctors
Who can get it?
If you’re pregnant and have Medi-Cal, you may qualify.
Ask your doctor about CPSP
OR
Call the Perinatal Care Network:
1-800-675-2229
They can help you find a doctor or check if you’re eligible.
Want to Provide CPSP Services?
If you're a doctor, nurse, midwife, or clinic that gives prenatal care and has a Medi-Cal number, you can apply to become a CPSP provider.
To apply or for more information about CPSP in Kern County, contact:
661-321-3000
CPSP is part of Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health (MCAH) and is required by the following authorities: Health & Safety Code, Sections 104560-104569 and 123475-12352; California Welfare and Institutions Code, Sections 14132(u) and 14134.5(a-j); and California Code of Regulations Title 22, Sections 51179-51179.0
